---
title: "Data 1 Project"
author(s): Jezel V., John E., Shelby T.
output: ioslides_presentation
date: "2026-04-08"
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = FALSE)
```

## 

```{r, echo=FALSE, out.width="70%", fig.align="center"}
knitr::include_graphics("https://logos-world.net/wp-content/uploads/2021/09/One-Piece-Logo-1999.png")
```

## Introduction 
In the vast world of anime and adventure, 


## About the Dataset
This specific dataframe (characters) has most (if not all) the characters from the famous Manga/Anime series "One Piece". It includes the characters name (ofc) along with their age, size, bounty (in berries), as well what crew the characters are a part of, & even if they have a devil fruit (as well as what kind with a description). The data set is extremely detailed and we were excited to work with it!

### Tools Used 

### Data Sources 
We were able to find multiple API's with One Piece information, but we ultimately decided to use the one below because it provided mostly all the information we were wanting to use for our project. Only thing is that like all data sets, we had to do some tidying, and some of the information was in French.... 

API Used: 
- https://api-onepiece.com/en/documentation 

### Tidying Data 
One Piece has a lot of data that we can work with, but it also has a lot of data that we have to tidy ):


```{r}
library(jsonlite)

## Character(s) Data 
url1 <- "https://api.api-onepiece.com/v2/characters/en"
characters <- fromJSON(url1)
characters_df <- as.data.frame(characters)
characters_df

## Locations Data
url2 <- "https://api.api-onepiece.com/v2/locates/en"
locations <- fromJSON(url2)
locations_df <- as.data.frame(locations)
locations_df


## Episodes Data 
url3 <- "https://api.api-onepiece.com/v2/episodes/en"
episodes <- fromJSON(url3)
episodes_df <- as.data.frame(episodes)
episodes_df


```



## Bounty

In One Piece, the more chaos you cause, the higher the bounty on your head is. The bounties reflect how dangerous each pirate is. 


Which character has the highest bounty in the series?
```{r}


```


Which character has the lowest bounty in the series?
```{r}

```


What is the average bounty between all pirates?
```{r}

```


Which "Blue" region produces pirates with higher average bounties?
```{r}
#can also be a ggplot 
```


Which crew has the highest bounty in total?
```{r}

```


What is the distribution of bounties? (high or low)
```{r}

```


Do the captains have a higher bounty than the fighters?





## Devil Fruits

How many pirates from each crew has a devil fruit?

How many characters have a devil fruit? 

Does having a devil fruit correlate to having a higher bounty?

How many different types of fruits are there? 
For each fruit, how many pirates contain one of the fruits? 
Do characters with devil fruits have bounties on their heads? 




## Islands

How many islands are in East Blue compared to the North Blue?

```{r}
library(dplyr)
library(ggplot2)

blue_counts <- locations_df %>%
  filter(sea_name %in% c("East Blue", "North Blue")) %>%
  count(sea_name)


ggplot(blue_counts, aes(x = sea_name, y = n)) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity") +
  labs(
    title = "Number of Islands: East Blue vs North Blue",
    x = "Sea",
    y = "Number of Islands"
  )
```

How many islands are in the Grand Line compared to the other regions 

How many islands are in each region? 

## Characters

ggplot

How many members are in the Strawhats crew?

```{r}

```

How many members are in the Big Mom crew?
How many members of Big Mom's crew are officers and lieutenants?

How many characters are captains of their crew? 

How many captains are there compared to navigators? 

How many characters are deceased? 
How many of those deceased characters have bounties on their heads?
